page: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Daldsv7 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, delivering 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes 2835 GB of local disk capacity and is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the D48alds_v7 demonstrates strong computational and memory capabilities. The AMD EPYC processor achieves a Passmark CPU score of 77885.39 and a database operations score of 28576.08, both placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reach top-tier performance in the top 10%, at 41.5 GB/sec and 18.1 GB/sec respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show a strong cached read speed of 5011.43 GB/sec and an uncached write speed of 102.98 GB/sec. For small-model LLM inference, prompt processing reaches a top-tier 4366.28 tokens/sec, while medium-model text generation registers at a strong 36.02 tokens/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the D48alds_v7 is its low memory density relative to its high compute core count, which makes it less suitable for memory-bound applications. However, this resource allocation provides high compute density and qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-heavy tasks that do not require massive RAM footprints. The inclusion of 2835 GB of local storage adds value for data-intensive operations. This server is suited for workloads such as web serving, database operations, compression tasks, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v7 is a compute-focused x86_64 server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs, 24 physical cores, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this instance is optimized for compute-heavy workloads. Benchmark results place its Passmark CPU score and database operations in the top 25%, while single-threaded compression tasks perform in the top 10%. The instance also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth and efficient small-to-medium LLM inference. The primary tradeoff is the low memory-to-core ratio, making it highly cost-efficient for CPU-bound tasks but less suitable for memory-intensive applications. It is suited for web serving, database operations, and data compression.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, delivering strong CPU and database performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v7 is an AMD-powered server with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, optimized for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered compute-optimized instance featuring forty-eight vCPUs, high-speed local storage, and strong database and compression performance.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz
96.0 GB of RAM providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio
2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-threaded compression and strong database benchmark performance
Suited for web serving, database operations, and lightweight LLM inference
